channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: I've been testing robot vacuums for years and these are the 5 Prime Day deals I'd recommend
item.description: Available at different price points, you can get a robovac that just vacuums to one that even cleans itself for up to 50% off.
item.dc:creator: sharmishta.sarkar@futurenet.com (Sharmishta Sarkar), TechRadar

item.title: AI pioneers win Nobel Prize in physics
item.description: Two pioneers of artificial intelligence — John Hopfield and Geoffrey Hinton — won the Nobel Prize in physics Tuesday for helping create the building blocks of machine learning that is revolutionizing the way we work and live but also creates new threats for humanity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: FTX Receives Court Approval for Bankruptcy Plan: A Victory for Creditors and Customers
item.description: FTX's court-approved bankruptcy plan allows the company to repay customers using up to $16.5 billion in assets recovered since its collapse.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Permitting council invests $15M in ‘surge capacity’ for agencies to manage infrastructure boom
item.description: Infrastructure projects supported by the Federal Permitting Council have seen a nearly 18-month reduction in the permitting process. The post Permitting council invests $15M in ‘surge capacity’ for agencies to manage infrastructure boom first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Julia language adds lower-overhead Memory type
item.description: A new version of the dynamically typed, high-performance Julia language for numerical computing has been released. Julia 1.11 features a new Memory type, a lower-level container that provides an alternative to Array. Downloadable from julialang.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A file extension bug in Microsoft 365 can delete Word documents
item.description: A bug in version 2409 of Microsoft 365 can cause Word documents to be deleted instead of saved, Microsoft has warned. The bug affects files that contain the # character or have file extensions in uppercase, such as .DOCX or .RTF.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles
item.description: The government needs to push the market in the direction of secure-by-design, including manufacturing, fielding and management of these new endpoints. The post IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: How AI is transforming the fan experience
item.description: For billions of people, being a sports fan is about community and identity. Some consider it to be what defines them. It is why they cheer for their teams or wear their jerseys. And it is a lifelong journey, marked by strong loyalty and dedication, making this a unique and interesting cohort.
item.dc:creator: Beshad Bezhadi, Fast Company

item.title: Apple's Open Platforms: Navigating Regulatory Changes and Enterprise Impacts
item.description: Regulatory actions force Apple to open its platforms, affecting IT admins and enterprise security.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: As global AI energy usage mounts, Nvidia claims efficiency gains of up to 100,000X
item.description: As concerns over AI energy consumption ratchet up, chip maker Nvidia is defending what it calls a steadfast commitment to sustainability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk, Tucker Carlson laugh about post discussing hypothetical Harris assassination
item.description: Conservative media pundit Tucker Carlson hosted tech mogul Elon Musk for an interview on his online video show this week, a sit-down that began with the two laughing about Musk's support for former President Trump and what he says was a "joke" he made about assassination attempts on political figure
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: From risk to reward: Mastering the art of adopting emerging technologies
item.description: One of the greatest things about working in technology is the surprise advancements that take the industry by storm.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Workspace vs. Microsoft 365: What’s the best office suite for business?
item.description: Once upon a time, Microsoft Office ruled the business world. By the late ’90s and early 2000s, Microsoft’s office suite had brushed aside rivals such as WordPerfect Office and Lotus SmartSuite, and there was no competition on the horizon.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Headhunted? That dream job could be a trap
item.description: Cybercriminals are increasingly targeting private accounts on LinkedIn and WhatsApp because they are less secure than business accounts, according to Europe cybersecurity agency Enisa’s latest threat landscape report.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cloud, edge e AI: come ottenere il massimo dalla loro integrazione
item.description: Il mondo dell’IT sta assistendo a una sempre più stretta convergenza tra cloud, edge e intelligenza artificiale.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This was the most disturbing part of Elon Musk’s Trump rally speech
item.description: Elon Musk achieved platinum meme status and got ridiculed on Saturday Night Live for gleefully leaping onstage at a Trump rally over the weekend. But a photo of Trump glowering as the Tesla tycoon flung himself skyward was far from Musk’s most egregious moment at that rally.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to use Window Tiling with macOS Sequoia
item.description: Window management on Macs gets an upgrade with macOS Sequoia, which introduces window tiling and makes it much easier to work on tasks within multiple applications by arranging windows as you please.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why evolving cyber threats mean small businesses are ransomware targets
item.description: Dark Matter’s powerful fictional short film based on real events, Butterfly, goes inside the experience of a small business as its world is turned upside down by ransomware.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nobel Prize in medicine honors 2 scientists for their discovery of microRNA
item.description: Two scientists have won the Nobel Prize in physiology or medicine for their discovery of microRNA, tiny bits of genetic material that serve as on and off switches inside cells that help control what the cells do and when they do it.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Niemann And Mike Corder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AirPods 4 vs Pro 2: Is the newer model worth it?
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson compares the AirPods 4 and the AirPods Pro 2. "For me, audio quality is the most essential factor in buying new headphones or earbuds," Kurt says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Understanding exit codes on Linux
item.description: Whenever you run a command in a Linux terminal, a numeric exit code is generated – even if you see no sign of it. The code reports the success or failure of the command. And, if the command fails, it provides a value that hints at the problem. If the exit code is 0, all is well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What’s new for Apple Intelligence?
item.description: Most Apple watchers may have noticed that the company’s iPhone 16 marketing really does put Apple Intelligence front and center, even though its home-baked breed of AaI (Artificial [Apple] Intelligence) isn’t available quite yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use extension methods in C#
item.description: In the C# programming language, extension methods enable you to extend the functionality of existing types without modifying them or deriving new types from them using inheritance.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo
item.description: The new executive assistant director for cybersecurity at CISA details how sharing threat intelligence more broadly pays off. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: IBM opens first quantum computing center in Europe
item.description: IBM unveiled its first IBM Quantum Data Center outside the United States – and its second ever – in Ehningen, Germany.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: iPhone's satellite messaging: A lifeline in Hurricane Helene's aftermath
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how iOS 18’s satellite messaging helps isolated residents seek help in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
